short_name: Evaluating the Impact of Adjuvant Use of Beta Blockers on Clinical Outcomes in Patients With Traumatic Brain Injury
title: Evaluating the Impact of Adjuvant Use of Beta Blockers on Clinical Outcomes in Patients With Traumatic Brain Injury

skipped_criteria: Inclusion: TBI must be detected on CT scan with moderate/severe classification — CT imaging result and injury severity classification are not available fields in the schema.

Inclusion: Patients must either have no other injuries or only minor injuries (mild IPFF, mild lung contusion, hemothorax/pneumothorax without hypoxia/tachypnea/respiratory distress, simple limb fractures) — associated injury type and severity are not available fields in the schema.
Exclusion: Patients on pre-injury beta-blocker therapy — prior medication use is not an available field in the schema.
Exclusion: Patients with any bronchospastic conditions — prior medical history/comorbidities are not available fields in the schema.
Exclusion: Patients with active acute coronary syndrome — prior medical history/comorbidities are not available fields in the schema.

Exclusion: TBI with GCS ≤ 13 with associated major injuries (moderate/marked IPFF requiring laparotomy, moderate/marked lung contusion/pneumothorax/hemothorax with hypoxia/tachypnea, compound fractures, open faciomaxillary trauma) — associated injury type and severity are not available fields in the schema.

Exclusion: Persistent shock defined as SBP < 100 mmHg, base deficit > 4, or oliguria, or HR < 60 bpm after more than one week of admission — the temporal qualifier 'after more than one week of admission' cannot be expressed in the schema; base deficit and oliguria are not available fields.
